description_long: This object is a dark, medieval-style sword with a straight, double-edged blade that tapers to a sharp point. The blade appears to be forged from metal, with subtle linear texturing along its length, suggesting a hand-crafted or aged finish. The crossguard is simple and straight, providing basic hand protection, and is also dark in color, matching the blade. The handle is cylindrical and ends in a rounded pommel, which is slightly larger and features a ribbed or grooved texture for grip. The entire sword has a matte, almost black finish, giving it a utilitarian and somewhat ominous appearance. There are no visible decorative elements or engravings, emphasizing function over ornamentation.
description: A dark, straight-bladed medieval sword with a simple crossguard and ribbed cylindrical handle, finished in matte black metal. The design is utilitarian, with no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the straight, double-edged blade, crossguard, and rounded pommel.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blade and handle, with the crossguard and pommel visible as narrow lines.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, and pommel from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the blade and handle, similar to view 1.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, ribbed, matte black metal, rounded pommel
